| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/the-collapse-of-trust-from-surveillance?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Dear friends,
Thank you for your patience while I've been away--spending time with family in Australia, resting, connecting with my nieces and nephews, watching my little ones play with their cousins and come to know their aunts and uncles. (It truly does take a village.) I've struggled with myself about whether I'm allowed time to recharge - but honestly, it's allowed me to return to you stronger. I hope you've been taking care of yourselves in the midst of it all: weaving your love into protesting, grieving, persisting. It's not an easy time to be awake, informed, and alive - but the alternative? Much worse. So let's count ourselves fortunate, take a deep breath of fresh air, grab hold of each other, and keep going.
If you're feeling stretched thin between heartbreak and rage, I'm with you. And I want to offer you that deep breath. A touchstone. A lens that might help us glimpse the larger pattern--not to disengage, but to reground and refocus.
Because something is quietly breaking beneath the surface of our politics. It isn't just about war or elections. It's about **trust** --and how, without it, even the most well-designed systems collapse.
### Trust is the First Casualty
We are watching trust dissolve--in media, in government, in science, in one another. Not just among extremists or conspiracy theorists, but across broad swaths of the public. The pandemic accelerated this erosion. Surveillance tech, algorithmic control, and AI-generated misinformation are speeding it up.
And the data backs this up: researchers measure societal trust levels across countries, often through longitudinal surveys asking people whether they believe "most people can be trusted." The answers to that single question correlate strongly with a wide array of outcomes--everything from GDP growth to health equity, from crime rates to resilience in crises. High-trust societies invest more in each other. They recover faster. They innovate more wisely. They hold the social fabric together.
The United States, once relatively high-trust, has been in a sharp decline since the 1970s. Polarization is a symptom, not a cause. Major drivers include increasing inequality, corruption scandals, media fragmentation, systemic racism, failures of accountability, and the commodification of attention and outrage. The result? A shared reality in freefall.
And as fear replaces trust, opportunists profit. The rise of authoritarianism, mass surveillance, and ideological rigidity isn't accidental--it thrives in the vacuum left by broken trust.
It's not just polarization. It's the slow hollowing-out of our collective sensemaking. The quiet panic of not knowing who or what to believe. And into that void steps the strongman, the algorithm, the black-and-white ideology. Anything that promises certainty.
But certainty is not the same as sovereignty.
The question now is: how do we begin to change this trajectory? How do we design for a different kind of trust--one that isn't naive or nostalgic, but resilient and regenerative?
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=161895230&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxODk1MjMwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDU0MzE5MDEsImV4cCI6MTc0ODAyMzkwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.6xtD-fdovWr0JbLlXWTcDFJBk_lmt-6Z2GPOZagfaIE)
### What If We Rebuilt Trust Differently?
The default response to distrust has been more control. More surveillance. More predictive policing. More data hoarding. But what if the answer isn't to double down on power--but to deepen _integrity_?
What if trust isn't something we impose--but something we design for?
Over the past month, I've been quietly working on something new. Something rooted in all the values this community has held dear--justice, foresight, compassion, integrity--but rendered in story form. It's called _The Havenseed Chronicles_ , and it's more than a novel. It's an offering, a map, a future that could be.
In the world I'm building--_The Havenseed Chronicles_ --this question is at the heart of the story. After a global collapse, trust doesn't magically return. It has to be reimagined. And it's not built on compliance, not anymore. It's built on **consent** , **transparency** , and **moral clarity**.
One of the core institutions in that world is called the Sorting Room--a place where truth is discerned not through coercion, but through empathy, memory, and AI systems trained not to control, but to *understand. *
### This Isn't Just Fiction. It's Foresight.
I'm not writing this series to escape politics. I'm writing it because the political tools we have aren't enough. We need stories that restore the imagination. That offer us templates for what could be.
Every week for the next six weeks, I'll be sharing glimpses into the Havenseed world--paired with analysis of the current moment, and invitations to imagine differently.
If you're ready to start now, here's a first taste of the story - a glimpse into one of the central dilemmas at the heart of Havenseed: the visceral tension of how we determine who is trustworthy, redeemable, and safe to belong to us--and who is not. In this scene, "wise AI" is employed to sort prisoners into three categories: wrongly imprisoned innocents; those who have caused harm but yearn for healing and community; and those who are, heartbreakingly, irredeemable in this lifetime, with whom no peace or coexistence can be safely forged. It is gut-wrenching, necessary, and luminous with moral clarity--an echo of questions we are already facing today:
Growing Our Thriving Future
[The Havenseed Chronicles: "The Sorting Room"](https://substack.com/redirect/03c214db-5b0d-46ec-b7b3-cca743ec114e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Context: We're now deep into Book Two of The Havenseed Chronicles, and the world has already changed. The Corona event--a global tech collapse triggered by a high-altitude EMP--fractured the imperial systems that once surveilled, imprisoned, and silenced dissent. But in the shadows of that collapse, a dangerous inheritance remains: thousands of human bein...
[Read more](https://substack.com/redirect/03c214db-5b0d-46ec-b7b3-cca743ec114e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
a day ago * Melissa Chaudhry
We're not powerless. But we _do_ need new blueprints.
As we continue the fight in the present moment--with all its urgency, weariness, and the ever-mounting cost--I hope you'll also join me in stretching our imaginations toward what comes after. We owe it to ourselves to envision a future in which the pain and crimes of empire and domination aren't buried or repeated, but held in full truth, remembered with reverence, and metabolized into the soil of a world that can finally sustain humanity in peace. That's the work of Havenseed--and the invitation I'm extending to you now.
With clarity and care, Melissa

name: Deb Haaland
subject: The first time I sat in the Speaker’s chair
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
I hope that one day, when a young woman of color sees photos of me in the Speaker’s chair (or in the governor’s office) they’ll think “I can do that too.”
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)
One thing I learned when I was first elected to the House of Representatives is that to conduct the business of the chamber, members take turns serving as the presiding officer.
We led by protecting the sacred right to vote with the _For the People Act_. When we were moving the _For the People Act_ through the House, it was the first time that I presided over the chamber and sat in the Speaker’s chair. And making it even more special was the fact that it was the first time _any_ Native American woman had presided over the chamber.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250423_1_speakerschair?refcode=em20250423-1510&:amount=5&refcode2=1510_400544_UTiqI8&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)
It was a moment that was filled with meaning for me, especially because John Lewis gave the closing argument on the bill.
I was standing in the Speaker’s chair and he was right in front of me explaining why _everybody_ needs access to the ballot box – and you know, it is something I’ll never forget.
John Lewis spent his life fighting for something we take for granted now, which is that all Americans, regardless of the color of their skin, must be allowed the right to vote. John Lewis put his life on the line and was beaten by Alabama state troopers so that he and those who came after him would be seen equally in the eyes of the law and have equal voice at the ballot box.
So to see him defending voting rights so passionately 50 years later on the House floor as a United States Congressperson was not only inspiring – it gives us all the perspective we need to continue this fight, which is far from over.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250423_1_speakerschair?refcode=em20250423-1510&:amount=5&refcode2=1510_400544_UTiqI8&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)
Republicans just passed the _SAVE Act_ in the House of Representatives – making it harder to vote and effectively rolling back the voting rights John Lewis fought so hard for.
I know one thing for certain: I’ll never stop fighting for the right to vote. And I hope that one day, when a young woman of color sees photos of me in the Speaker’s chair (or in the governor’s office) they’ll think “I can do that too.”
That’s part of why I’m in public service and running for governor. I want to leave the ladder down so that every voter can make their voice heard in our democracy and those who have been underrepresented have the same access to vote as everyone else. It’s about fairness and respect.
For too long, the system hasn’t worked for everyday people the way it should. As governor, I’ll tackle our biggest challenges head-on and fight to make life better for _all_ the people of New Mexico.
**[But I can’t do it without you. If you’re with me in the fight to build a New Mexico for _everyone_ , will you pitch in $5 or any amount you can to support my campaign today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250423_1_speakerschair?amount=5&refcode=em20250423-1510&refcode2=1510_400544_UTiqI8&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250423_1_speakerschair?amount=5&refcode=em20250423-1510&refcode2=1510_400544_UTiqI8&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)**
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250423-1&t=2&refcode2=1510_400544_UTiqI8&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=1510%2E400544%2EUTiqI8)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)

With 7 days until our deadline, we’re less than 40% toward our goal.. | | | | | ---|--- Did you know that a can help fight Trump’s agenda in states nationwide? Before you consider donating, let me explain why your gift today is so critical → Next Tuesday, Democrats are competing in a special election for a seat in the Minnesota Senate. This election will be a chance to strengthen Democratic power. This November, all 100 seats in the Virginia House and the governorship are on the ballot, giving Democrats a real shot at a trifecta. Last quarter, our Republican counterparts raised a record-breaking $13 million to build power in the states. We need to hit our $125,000 April fundraising goal to compete against the GOP’s mega-donors and hold our ground in the states. It’s essential that state Democrats have the resources they need to win against their extreme opponents in upcoming elections nationwide. With just seven days until our April deadline, we’re less than 40% toward our goal. | Hi Peter, **Earlier this month, Floridians went to the polls and showed how they think Trump is doing. name: Emma at Evergreen
subject: Check out our new look on social media 👀
body:
Introducing: Hope This Helps 🚀
| | | | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3522334/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Hi there,

As the Trump regime’s attacks on working Americans continue to dominate social media, it’s easy to feel like the odds are stacked against us.

**But here’s some good news:** there are still many meaningful ways to protect our communities, take on the climate crisis, and fight back against economic injustice.

There are people with solutions who are mobilizing right now, building power and fighting back against the greedy interests that would hurt us. |
---
| | | [ ](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3522335/87SdQ4wZ)
---
**The big news:**

To help create a better _climate_ online (see what I did there?) to talk about all this, we’re giving some of our social media channels a refresh, rebranding ourselves on Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and YouTube as **_Hope this Helps._**
---
---
| Rest assured, we’re still the same Evergreen with the same mission to build a thriving and just clean energy future, but we’ll be talking about things a little differently on these channels to engage new people in the movement.

[**Here’s a sneak peek!**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3522336/87SdQ4wZ)**👇** |
| [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3522337/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| **_Hope This Helps_** will feature the kind of fun content you want on your social media feeds. It will meet people where so many of them are right now—overwhelmed by energy costs, disillusioned with politics, and mad about the direction our country is heading. It will shine a light on the people in power who are really holding all the cards and provide the solutions to hold them accountable. In doing so, we hope to bring more people into the fights we need to win to protect working people from economic and climate catastrophe.

**But we need your help!**

You can help us build power right now by sharing our content with your networks. If you’re not already following us on social media, **please follow _Hope This Helps_ on the following channels, make sure to click the option to “get alerts” when we post updates, and share some posts:** |
---
|
### ⭐️ [Instagram](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3522338/87SdQ4wZ)

clean_body: Introducing: Hope This Helps 🚀 | | | | | ---|---|--- | Hi there, As the Trump regime’s attacks on working Americans continue to dominate social media, it’s easy to feel like the odds are stacked against us. But here’s some good news: there are still many meaningful ways to protect our communities, take on the climate crisis, and fight back against economic injustice. There are people with solutions who are mobilizing right now, building power and fighting back against the greedy interests that would hurt us. | | | | The big news: To help create a better climate online (see what I did there?) to talk about all this, we’re giving some of our social media channels a refresh, rebranding ourselves on Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and YouTube as Hope this Helps. | Rest assured, we’re still the same Evergreen with the same mission to build a thriving and just clean energy future, but we’ll be talking about things a little differently on these channels to engage new people in the movement. 👇 | | | ---|---|--- | Hope This Helps will feature the kind of fun content you want on your social media feeds. It will meet people where so many of them are right now—overwhelmed by energy costs, disillusioned with politics, and mad about the direction our country is heading. It will shine a light on the people in power who are really holding all the cards and provide the solutions to hold them accountable. In doing so, we hope to bring more people into the fights we need to win to protect working people from economic and climate catastrophe. But we need your help! You can help us build power right now by sharing our content with your networks. If you’re not already following us on social media, please follow Hope This Helps on the following channels, make sure to click the option to “get alerts” when we post updates, and share some posts: | | ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ | | Thanks for all your support of Evergreen thus far. I look forward to seeing you engage with us more on social media as we embark on this exciting new endeavor! | | In solidarity, | | | | Emma Hennessey Digital Lead - Social Media Evergreen Collaborative * | Evergreen is building the ambitious, actionable policy roadmap for an all-out mobilization to defeat climate change—and to create millions of jobs in a thriving, just, and inclusive clean energy future. None of this would be possible without your support. | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- 1250 Connecticut Ave NW, Suite 700, Washington DC 20036 | | content © 2025 Evergreen Collaborative. All rights reserved.

subject: The Republicans' budget fiasco

Team, The Republican budget fiasco continues as Mike Johnson and House Republicans threaten to gut Medicaid so they can pay for their $4 trillion tax cut for the wealthy. As families struggle to pay for the increasing costs of groceries, Republicans are debating how big a tax break they're giving to the rich and powerful. I will vehemently oppose their plan, which is completely out of touch with hard-working families making ends meet. I'm waging this fight all while Republicans are waging one on me. They are targeting my seat in NJ-09 as a top-targeted race for them to try to flip — and in doing so, grow their House majority. name: Team Heinrich
subject: Keep public lands in public hands
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106373219/541311908/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YjAwNDkzYy03MzIwLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vT176w_mvt7mytOvAM3YPV2PrSC9O0HX6DFK-uVxT0U=&emci=3c00493c-7320-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=4b00493c-7320-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=252045)

Team, Donald Trump and his Republican allies will do anything to pay for his tax cuts for billionaires — even sell off our public lands. In January, House Republicans changed the rules to make it easier to sell public lands to the highest bidder. Then, Trump and Elon Musk fired thousands and thousands of Forest Service employees, Park Service rangers, National Fish and Wildlife habitat specialists — you name it, all to make it harder to manage our federal public lands. Now, Republicans are scheming to sell public lands to pay for tax cuts for the rich. Let’s be clear: **Selling off public lands would mean everyday folks could no longer access the places they hike, fish, spend time with their families, or even their ancestral sacred sites. ** That’s why Martin is fighting back to protect our public lands and ensure the next generation can enjoy the lands that help define our nation.

**[If you agree with Martin that public lands should be kept in public hands, add your name to our petition today>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106373220/541311909/2137156591?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YjAwNDkzYy03MzIwLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vT176w_mvt7mytOvAM3YPV2PrSC9O0HX6DFK-uVxT0U=&emci=3c00493c-7320-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=4b00493c-7320-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=252045)** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106373221/541311910/2137156591?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YjAwNDkzYy03MzIwLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vT176w_mvt7mytOvAM3YPV2PrSC9O0HX6DFK-uVxT0U=&emci=3c00493c-7320-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=4b00493c-7320-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=252045)

I’m running for Governor of Michigan to take us from the bottom ten to the top ten in public education.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=245%2E181023%2EUw83se)
I’ve heard it from families in Grand Rapids, Detroit, and Marquette: They want their kids to attend good, well-funded public schools and receive a strong education that will set them up for lifelong success.
I want the same thing, which is why I’m fighting to give every kid in Michigan a strong education — regardless of their ZIP code or how much money their family makes.
**[Sign on to support my plans to make Michigan the best place to be a kid and go to school >>](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/public-education-pet?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=245%2E181023%2EUw83se)**
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/public-education-pet?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=245%2E181023%2EUw83se)**
As a former educator and daughter of two public school teachers, I know firsthand how important a strong public education is. My love for education and the incredible teachers who do that hard work day-in and day-out has grounded every step of my career.
At 35, I was appointed dean of Wayne State University Law School, becoming the youngest woman to lead an accredited law school in American history. I worked to raise money to ensure all students could pay for their education, lower costs by freezing tuition and expanding scholarships, and build a team rooted in honesty, integrity, and service to people.
**Now, I’m running for Governor of Michigan to take us from the bottom ten to the top ten in public education.**
To be clear — we have our work cut out for us. My GOP opponents are running on an anti-public education agenda, and they’re backed by anti-public education donor and Trump’s first Secretary of Education, Betsy DeVos.
**[But I’ve been standing up to bullies and billionaires who want to take us backward my entire career, and I’m not going to stop now. I’m running to make sure every kid in Michigan receives a world-class education. Join me by adding your name today.](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/public-education-pet?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=245%2E181023%2EUw83se)**
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/public-education-pet?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250423_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=245%2E181023%2EUw83se)**
A strong education is the foundation for a strong future. Let’s fight to make sure our kids are set up for lifelong success.
— Jocelyn

Peter, it's Karen. My time as a legislator showed me that these are the most chaotic, but important days of the legislative session. The conference committee calendar is underway – the time when dead bills can be revived, and ideas taken up without much, if any, public input. With the $2 billion budget revenue shortfall now forecasted, it’s more important than ever that our lawmakers get this right, and don’t slash essential programs for vulnerable Hoosiers. We’ve already seen dangerous changes – such as removal of caseloads limits for DCS workers, and the dissolution of one of our rural school districts. name: No Dem Left Behind
subject: Economy Watch: April 23rd, 2025
body:
The impact of tariffs is being felt in every corner of the country.
| | | | | |
---
**_Economy Watch_**
---
Hello, friend! Welcome back to _Economy Watch,_ our new weekly newsletter breaking down what’s really happening in our economy. I’m Zoltan Nemeth, and every Wednesday, alongside my colleagues Olya Makarova and Hassan Martini, we’ll bring you the latest economic developments and what they mean for working Americans. This week, all eyes are on Trump’s reckless tariffs, and the ripple effect they’re having across the stock market and beyond. **Let’s get into it.**
---
| | | | **Tariff Update**
---
|
---
Trump's massive tariffs are taking a toll on everyday Americans, hitting them directly in the wallet. This impact goes beyond the stock market, which has taken another dive this week. It's being felt in grocery stores, small businesses, and virtually every corner of the nation. If these tariffs stay in place, the cost of essentials — furniture, clothing, groceries, electronics — will climb dramatically, just as household budgets are already stretched thin. Trump has imposed a 145% tariff on goods from China. That’s not a typo. Everything from your next pair of sneakers to your kids’ school supplies could cost nearly double by summer. Retailers are warning that once current inventory runs out, prices will spike. **It doesn’t stop there.** We import most of our coffee from Brazil and Colombia. We import our chocolate, vanilla, and sugar from Latin America, Indonesia, and the Philippines. All of it will now come with a massive price tag. Even American-made products will be affected. Why? Because domestic brands raise prices to match imports — and to protect their profits. As a result, small businesses **_(the backbone of America’s economy)_** are hanging by a thread. Most don’t have the working capital to survive tariffs this steep. Local restaurants in Pennsylvania. Toy stores in Oregon. Boutiques in Wisconsin. They are all impacted. These are people's livelihoods, and under Trump’s plan, many of them are headed for bankruptcy. An economist at Apollo Global Management didn’t mince words: if these tariffs stay in place, “we will absolutely have a recession in 2025.” **_Let that sink in:_** → 90% chance of a two-quarter economic contraction → 4% drop in GDP → Massive retail bankruptcies → Job losses across the country This is the real cost of Trump. While billionaires enjoy tax breaks, working families will pay more for socks, soap, sugar, and cereal. If you think this can’t get worse, it can. Trump has only paused some tariffs for 90 days, just long enough to calm the stock market before it crashes again. Meanwhile, consumer confidence is plummeting, and CEOs across the country are sounding the alarm. **We have a real chance to put a check on this recklessness in the 2026 midterms. Stay tuned with NDLB to see how you can make difference.**
---
|
---
In solidarity, Zoltan Nemeth
---

---
| [$26 for 2026](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2x9uk7-wev1w7-kyd76rh2/)

clean_body: The impact of tariffs is being felt in every corner of the country. | | | | | | Economy Watch Hello, friend! Welcome back to Economy Watch, our new weekly newsletter breaking down what’s really happening in our economy. I’m Zoltan Nemeth, and every Wednesday, alongside my colleagues Olya Makarova and Hassan Martini, we’ll bring you the latest economic developments and what they mean for working Americans. This week, all eyes are on Trump’s reckless tariffs, and the ripple effect they’re having across the stock market and beyond. Let’s get into it. | | | | Tariff Update | Trump's massive tariffs are taking a toll on everyday Americans, hitting them directly in the wallet. This impact goes beyond the stock market, which has taken another dive this week. It's being felt in grocery stores, small businesses, and virtually every corner of the nation. If these tariffs stay in place, the cost of essentials — furniture, clothing, groceries, electronics — will climb dramatically, just as household budgets are already stretched thin. Trump has imposed a 145% tariff on goods from China. That’s not a typo. Everything from your next pair of sneakers to your kids’ school supplies could cost nearly double by summer. Retailers are warning that once current inventory runs out, prices will spike. It doesn’t stop there. We import most of our coffee from Brazil and Colombia. We import our chocolate, vanilla, and sugar from Latin America, Indonesia, and the Philippines. All of it will now come with a massive price tag. Even American-made products will be affected. Why? Because domestic brands raise prices to match imports — and to protect their profits. As a result, small businesses (the backbone of America’s economy) are hanging by a thread. Most don’t have the working capital to survive tariffs this steep. Local restaurants in Pennsylvania. Toy stores in Oregon. Boutiques in Wisconsin. They are all impacted. These are people's livelihoods, and under Trump’s plan, many of them are headed for bankruptcy. An economist at Apollo Global Management didn’t mince words: if these tariffs stay in place, “we will absolutely have a recession in 2025.” Let that sink in: → 90% chance of a two-quarter economic contraction → 4% drop in GDP → Massive retail bankruptcies → Job losses across the country This is the real cost of Trump. While billionaires enjoy tax breaks, working families will pay more for socks, soap, sugar, and cereal. If you think this can’t get worse, it can. Trump has only paused some tariffs for 90 days, just long enough to calm the stock market before it crashes again. Meanwhile, consumer confidence is plummeting, and CEOs across the country are sounding the alarm. We have a real chance to put a check on this recklessness in the 2026 midterms. Stay tuned with NDLB to see how you can make difference. | In solidarity, Zoltan Nemeth | | | | We appreciate your support in this pivotal time. | | | | | No Dem Left Behind PAC P.O. Box 15320 Washington, D.C. 20003 | You are subscribed to this email as dpwillis67@gmail.com. Click here to modify your or .

Sign on to our emergency petition IMMEDIATELY to CANCEL and DEFUND Trump’s Birthday Military Parade! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cancel-parade-v2/?source=VPP_20250423_EMPET_E3_T0_NS_RS&t=7&akid=19594%2E878179%2EfoRTvI) | [TELL CONGRESS: DEFUND TRUMP’S MILITARY PARADE »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cancel-parade-v2/?source=VPP_20250423_EMPET_E3_T0_NS_RS&t=8&akid=19594%2E878179%2EfoRTvI)

A military parade is straight out of an authoritarian dictatorship.

**This is a slap in the face to the American people. We won’t stand for it, so we’re calling on Congress to act IMMEDIATELY.**

[**Join 25,000 Democrats in our virtual protest and add your name NOW to cancel Trump’s military parade!**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cancel-parade-v2/?source=VPP_20250423_EMPET_E3_T0_NS_RS&t=12&akid=19594%2E878179%2EfoRTvI)

Hey friend,
Next week, April 30, marks 100 days of Trump in office.
Trump’s second presidency has brought us to unprecedented lows. Trump has tanked our economy, given free rein to an unelected billionaire to fire thousands of federal workers, and openly defied a unanimous Supreme Court order.
Republicans are tripping over themselves to bow at Trump and Elon’s feet, they are failing to do the basics of the job — protecting our democracy by checking the White House’s overreach.
We can take back control, to save Medicaid and Social Security, when we flip the House and Senate. Democrats cannot take back Congress without Nevada and we can’t win the Silver State without your support.

Trump has tanked our economy, given free rein to an unelected billionaire to fire thousands of federal workers, and openly defied a unanimous Supreme Court order. Republicans are tripping over themselves to bow at Trump and Elon’s feet, they are failing to do the basics of the job — protecting our democracy by checking the White House’s overreach. We can take back control, to save Medicaid and Social Security, when we flip the House and Senate. Democrats cannot take back Congress without Nevada and we can’t win the Silver State without your support. | | Hi folks,Not even two weeks ago, Michael launched his campaign to be the next Governor of Colorado, and we’ve been absolutely thrilled by the response. We’ve felt Coloradans’ enthusiasm from every corner of our state; from the people chipping in a few bucks where they can to the Colorado leaders who have lined up to endorse Michael’s campaign—it’s been exciting, to say the least._And, in one week, our campaign hits our first big test—our end-of-month fundraising deadline._ **[It’s vital that we prove our momentum will last for the long haul. Team, we’re going to need your help to make this happen. name: Patriot Polls
subject: Do you still stand with President Trump?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Can we have a minute of your time?

| | | Peter,

President Trump's powerful joint address to Congress left the Democrats **REELING.** Their behavior was a stark reminder that they are entirely out of touch with the American people.

As President Trump honored American heroes, the Democrats sat in stunned silence, refusing to applaud for the very people they claim to represent. Steelworkers, farmers, grieving mothers, and a young boy battling brain cancer - all ignored by the Democrats.

This shameful display exposed the Democrats' true priorities: **politics over people.**

The contrast between the two parties couldn't be clearer. President Trump and the Republicans lead with competence, sanity, and a pro-American agenda. Meanwhile, the Democrats are descending into chaos, driven by incompetence and irrationality.

Democrats simply cannot admit that President Trump's America First agenda is working, which is why we want YOUR opinion, Peter. [**Please take a minute to complete our Agenda 47 Survey and let us know if you still stand with President Trump:**](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-uPLZA)
---
[ **TAKE THE AGENDA 47 SURVEY HERE**](https://trk.emails.troydowning._I1EgjNJX389Q3grwiw)
---
Thank you for your continued support of the America First movement.

Best regards,
Patriot Polls

name: MTG
subject: Leaked Democrat emails revealed
body:
Democrats survive off of dirty political games, and they love dragging me into them.
| | | | Friend – Jon Ossoff – Georgia’s failed liberal posterboy – has been fundraising off of attacking me for _years..._**_And I’ve had enough._**
Just minutes ago, I was able to read over a leaked email that he signed — _and it confirmed what I have suspected all along:_

**Today is the 1 year anniversary that we launched** _**Let 's Address This with Qasim Rashid.**_**** I wrote on April 23, 2024:
### Global human rights are at historic lows, and the United States is among the worst offenders. By subscribing to Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid, you're helping turn the tide to protect human rights for all.
In the last 1 year, you have made Let's Address This _that_ platform that more than 130,000 people rely on for daily, critical, and accurate information on human rights. I am grateful to this community. I will always cherish the tens of thousands of notes I received from each of you offering condolences for the passing of my father, Maulana Abdur Rashid Yahya. I am grateful for the solidarity as we collectively navigate the incessant attacks on our human rights and dignity. And I will always smile that the most commented on feature of my weekly recaps are the Dad Jokes :)
In short, I am truly honored for your support. Your support has elevated _Let 's Address This_ to accomplish the following:
* Publish **393 unique pieces** on human rights, justice, and democracy.
* Our podcast, The Qasim Rashid Show, has earned more than **80K downloads**.
* Sent more than **100K petition signatures to Congress** to oppose unjust and discriminatory legislation.
* Earn the trust of **130K subscribers and followers** to our platform.
* Read by more than **20M readers** in just our first year. (Yes, Million!)
* Rank **Top 71 Politics platforms** in the entire Substack universe.

**And you graciously shared why you subscribed to Let 's Address This:**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/94f20fe0-3b02-4c67-bbc1-c598df1e47e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
These are but a snapshot of the thousands of notes I receive from you each month. Please know I read and cherish them all.
## So Where Do We Go Next?
With you leading the way, we continue to build for justice. To do that, we're expanding how to access and organize our content. Therefore, I have partnered with an amazing org called Oak to streamline my work into one spot. So, if you find yourself wondering about a piece I wrote, but unable to locate it, you'll find it quickly and easily here:
[All of Qasim's Content In One Spot](https://substack.com/redirect/7745a001-ac3c-4e27-b203-35200c1e3036?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Likewise, I ask you to choose at least 1 of my 3 calls to action and elevate _Let 's Address This_ to the next level (especially as the need increases to build against the growing intolerances we are seeing today).
1. **Jump in.** Become a paid subscriber at a cup of coffee ($6) a month or $50 a year. We are not funded by billionaires. We are supported by We the People. For context, if you subscribed at $50/year just 1 year ago, you would have spent a whopping 13 cents per post.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Sxb_PzS1OBUY-YyfG3N_tLf47KYZFs1sICn3haGTycw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=161986032)
2. **Grow our community.** Gift a subscription to a friend or loved one. The best way to build our movement is _each one reach one_. Let's grow from 130K to 260K.
[Give a gift subscription](https://substack..Sxb_PzS1OBUY-YyfG3N_tLf47KYZFs1sICn3haGTycw?&gift=true)
3. **Maximize our reach.** Share this post on your social media, in your family group chat, or simply like and comment. Engage and help boost its visibility.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=161986032&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxOTg2MDMyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDU0Mzg1NjMsImV4cCI6MTc0ODAzMDU2MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.603-RqzmP0NRfuIy8_BBcWzqqa6n5lJhXkpTu2sMbj8)

## **A Final Word**
**As we build into year two and beyond, I 'd like to reaffirm these three promises:**
1. My writing and content will _never_ sit behind a paywall. It is, and will remain, accessible to all people regardless of whether they are a paid subscriber or free subscriber.
2. My content will always be accurate, and if I get something wrong, I will absolutely follow up with a correction. I do not profess perfection, but you can count on my commitment to hold truth and justice as my North Star.
3. My standard will remain to uphold justice above all else, while elevating calls to action to combat injustice. My writing will not only shed light on the struggles harming our nation, but also on the practical steps we can take to rectify injustice.

As corporate media continues to capitulate to fascism, as billionaires line up behind a fascist for personal benefit at our expense, and as too many politicians remain cowardly silent--my mission remains clear: **Uphold absolute justice and human rights in all I do**. I will continue to so without billionaire support. Instead, I will do so with the people, by the people, and for the people. **This is why I seek your support.** Your support of _Let 's Address This_ is an investment to demand justice and universal human rights. Thank you for your support during this last year. I look forward to another brilliant year of success beyond.
Subscribe, share, and let's remain relentless in our mission for a more perfect Union.
**Thank you for your trust. ❤️**
Sincerely,
Qasim

Friend, I’m reaching out today because, despite everything that has happened since Trump took office, we need to make sure our fundraising stays on track.
**So, I’m hoping you can chip in $5 today and help me continue to stand strong for our progressive values.**
$5 because for the last three months Elon Musk and Republicans have been personally attacking me from all sides.
$5 because we need to build a nationwide, grassroots movement that can oppose Trump’s authoritarian rule and far-right agenda.
$5 because we’ve never taken a dime from corporate Super PACs or far-right mega-donors to fund this campaign.
I know $5 doesn’t sound like much in the face of billionaire oligarchs, but trust me, it all adds up. And we have seen time and time again that a grassroots funded campaign can beat **_anyone**_ if we stay organized.

name: Americans for Responsible Innovation
subject: Press Conference: Legal Experts Endorse TAKE IT DOWN Act
body:
Zephyr Teachout, Slade Bond, others discuss bill’s constitutional foundation

| | | | | |
---
| | | | |
# Press Conference: Legal Experts
**Endorse TAKE IT DOWN Act**
---
| On Monday, April 28, Americans for Responsible Innovation (ARI) will host a virtual press conference on the TAKE IT DOWN Act with top legal experts including Professor Zephyr Teachout, and former DOJ official Slade Bond.
Together, these legal experts will share their perspectives on the constitutionality of the TAKE IT DOWN Act, the bill’s impact on speech and non-consensual intimate imagery, and enforcement. Press conference participants will answer questions from attendees as the bill moves towards an imminent vote in the House of Representatives.
---
| [Register](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=011e18ac07&e=a07d75591f)
---
[|
---](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=a3e0d84936&e=a07d75591f)
| | | | | | | | | | | Monday’s panel includes experts from across the legal and tech policy community, including:
* **Zephyr Teachout** , Professor of Law, Fordham Law School
* **Slade Bond** , Chair of Public Policy and Legislative Affairs, Cuneo Gilbert & LaDuca LLP, former Principal Deputy Assistant AG for Legislative Affairs at DOJ
* **Stefan Turkheimer** , Vice President for Public Policy, RAINN/SVPA
* **Adam Billen** , Vice President of Public Policy, Encode

[**Register here.**](https://responsibleinnovation.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c0dd7c48fa7fb81cbc8185a&id=8a1cf6dcd7&e=a07d75591f)

name: Shyam Raman
subject: Why Virginia should matter to every Democrat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
I’ll cut right to the chase – Virginia’s statewide elections in November 2025 are deeply important for retaining Democrats’ momentum as we enter a second Trump term. &nb | Hi Peter,

I’m Shyam Raman, Executive Director of the Democratic Party of Virginia.

I’ll cut right to the chase – **Virginia’s statewide elections in November 2025 are deeply important for retaining Democrats’ momentum as we enter a second Trump term.** If successful, electing a strong Democratic governor and general assembly would be a key referendum on Trump and the GOP.

Not only that, but we have a responsibility to keep Virginia blue, as we’re the only state in the Southeast without strict restrictions on reproductive care.

[_**We have a critical fundraising deadline coming up at the end of the quarter, and we need to demonstrate we have the grassroots power to fight and WIN. Will you chip in to support the most important election in 2025? Make a split donation between our party and Fight for Progress!**_](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=WVbgGwM5vjos&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=25)
---
| | [ $3 ](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=DNckA5xQA3KG&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=3)
---
| [ $5 ](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=icEddFfTWBxW&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=5)
---
| [ $10 ](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=CZ0AkG9nnJae&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=10)
---
| [ $15 ](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=VrLOSl7beKp7&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=15)
---
| [ $25 ](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=o9c7LwEA2R9z&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=25)
---
| [ $50 ](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=WeDHNlador9A&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=50)
---
We can be the start of the Democratic comeback if we work together. We cannot stop fighting, and we cannot abandon our deepest-held beliefs: that our nation should be a place where everyone has the tools to succeed and the freedom to choose how to live their lives.

The current administration represents the worst of us. They not only threaten our standing on the world stage but represent a politics driven by fear and hatred of the other.

[_**So please, let’s win a key Democratic win for us in 2025. Chip in to support our work here in Virginia and Fight for Progress ahead of our critical end-of-quarter deadlines to ensure we can rebuild and show that the American voters already reject the GOP’s extremism.**_](https://fight4progress.org/vadems?t=lkqS4dPHTGuG&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-22_1&amount=25)

Thank you,
Shyam Raman
Executive Director, Virginia Democratic Party

## Martin Daubney in FURIOUS debate over ‘attack on English nationalists’
Martin Daubney has erupted in a heated debate on GB News today over Prime Minister Keir Starmer's St George's Day comments about reclaiming the English flag. The fiery exchange came after Keir Starmer called for the English flag to be "reclaimed" from rioters who caused destruction following last summer's Southport stabbings. John-Henry Westen reflects on the passing of Pope Francis and the profound spiritual challenges that marked his pontificate. While urging prayers for the repose of his soul, Westen examines seven major controversies that shaped this era of the Church—from the Pachamama incident to restrictions on the Latin Mass. unpopular outcasts like lepers and prostitutes, he might have won more
support from white working-class Judeans.
Where did Pope Francis go wrong with America's right-wing Catholic
bloggers? For one thing, his celibacy was voluntary.

### **Nothing Says Strength Like Using Tanks to Forget the Limpness of Your Manhood --And Presidency**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bc2a7135-c01f-4540-85ea-9836b012b0e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Trump's dream of a little tank parade to feel manly
Donald Trump is once again fantasizing about tanks rumbling down the streets of D.C., not to stop a coup like the domestic-terror attack he helped _instigate_ , but to feel like a boy. A real, real boy. I know he doesn't get the concept of democracy--much like tariffs, multiplication, multi-syllabic words, vegetables, a fitting suit or running a business that doesn't go arse-up, to name a few--so I'll try to explain it to him.
Let's learn a little history, Donny, you mega-jingoistic cod-piece. **Our Founders feared standing armies** , because they knew a permanent military was a military to be used. Often, j _ust because it was there_. And against its own citizens if/when the Scylla and Charybdes of Presidents came along. _And here you are._
> _What, sir, is the use of a militia? It is to prevent the establishment of a standing army, the bane of liberty. ...Whenever Governments mean to invade the rights and liberties of the people, they always attempt to destroy the militia, in order to raise an army upon their ruins._
> _- Elbridge Gerry, Fifth Vice President of the United States_
The National Guard wasn't even created until 1903. Militias carried a lot of the load (ya know, those "well-regulated" ones f _or whom the Second Amendment was actually_ _intended_? As opposed to your next-door neighbor Billy Bob with a few cases of dismissed-domestic battery, fixin' to get himself 47 AR-15s, just because).
Even between World Wars I & II, our military was badly underfunded because that fear remained in our DNA. Dwight Eisenhower, upon leaving the presidency in 1961, infamously warned about the "military industrial complex." You know that thing from toxic treason toads like Erik Prince and Paul Manafort greatly, financially benefitted...

She entered and observed the male suspect, later identified as 23-year-old **Mitchell Blake Reinacher** , standing in the hallway over the body of a man. The man was bleeding heavily from knife wounds to his chest.

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas measles outbreak could reach 1,000 cases for first time in decades, experts say,** " via Houston Chronicle's **Evan MacDonald** \-- "When measles began spreading earlier this year in northwest Texas, it didn't take long for the state to reach 50 cases for the first time in three decades.

That milestone was a far cry from the 1,097 measles cases that Texas reported back in 1992, but the outbreak has continued to grow. The Texas Department of State Health Services reported 624 cases on Tuesday, including the deaths of two school-aged children.

Experts said the outbreak is difficult to forecast for a variety of reasons, but it has not shown any signs of abating. If the virus continues to spread, Texas could reach or**** exceed 1,000 measles cases in a single year for the first time in more than 30 years.

"I think there's at least a 50% chance we get up there. Maybe higher," said**Catherine Troisi** , an infectious disease epidemiologist at the UTHealth Houston School of Public Health. "I hope I'm wrong. But we have all the ingredients ... for this to just keep burning."

Texas health officials have reported about 50 new cases of measles per week since Valentine's Day, aside from a two-week surge in late March and early April, according to DSHS data. The state reported 121 new cases during the week that ended March 21 and 81 new cases during the week that ended April 4.

Additionally, experts have cautioned that measles cases are likely being underreported in Texas and other states. A U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention scientist said last week that the agency believes "[quite a large amount of cases](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad9e8e9-978f-43b7-8cbf-7aae21433250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" are not being reported, as many families have not sought testing or treatment.

If the outbreak continues at its current pace, Texas could eclipse 1,000 cases by early summer. The total could climb much higher if the outbreak continues to spread throughout 2025, which public health officials have warned is possible.

It's difficult to predict how the outbreak will evolve for a variety of reasons, said **R emy Pasco**, the associated director of modeling for epiENGAGE, an infectious disease modeling partnership led by researchers from the University of Texas at Austin and the University of Massachusetts Amherst. But he believes Texas is likely to reach 1,000 cases, even if that happens gradually over the course of several months.

"When there's a steady number of cases every week, we don't know whether we're close to the end or not," said Pasco, also a research associate at the University of Colorado at Boulder. "Which is the frustrating part."

Texas routinely reported tens of thousands of measles cases per year before the measles, mumps and rubella vaccine was introduced in 1963. Widespread use of the MMR vaccine caused infections to plummet across the United States, but a measles outbreak among school-aged children prompted federal health officials to recommend a second dose in 1989.

Texas reported 4,409 cases of measles in 1990, the state's highest total in nearly two decades. The statewide total dropped to 294 cases in 1991 before rising to 1,097 in 1992.

Since then, the highest number of cases that Texas reported in a full year had been 49, back in 1996. This year's outbreak surpassed that total just after Valentine's Day.

There are no signs the current outbreak is slowing, because the state has been reporting a steady number of new cases each week, said Rebecca Fischer, an assistant professor in the department of epidemiology and biostatistics at the Texas A&M University School of Public Health.

"With measles, that is not a good thing," Fischer said. "With any contagious disease, we always want to see (case numbers) going down."

The state also has ongoing measles transmission in 10 counties, and larger cities like Lubbock and El Paso have begun to see infections.

Experts noted that many Texans remain susceptible to measles because they are not protected by the MMR vaccine or a prior infection. Many Texas communities have "pockets" of such individuals at schools, day cares or other settings the virus could spread, experts said.

"I think there's no shortage of folks, unfortunately, who could get measles," Troisi said." [Houston Chronicle](https://substack.com/redirect/e92aee2a-12e1-4c38-8c0c-7b58071e579f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

"**A woman hugs the man who fatally shot her brother and 22 more in a racist attack at a Texas Walmart,** " Houston Chronicle's **John Wayne Ferguson** \-- "Speaking to the gunman who killed her brother and 22 other people, **Yolanda Tinajero** did not raise her voice or condemn him for his racist attack at a Walmart in 2019. Instead she told him Tuesday that she forgave him, and wished she could give him a hug.

The judge, in a surprising turn in an El Paso courtroom, allowed her to do just that.

Their brief embrace -- while Patrick Crusius was still shackled -- was among many emotionally charged moments during two days of impact statements given by victims' family members and survivors.

[Some described](https://substack.com/redirect/4002c90c-5b41-4815-8f26-fe325f4c1bcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) their pain and devastation while others assured him the community had met his hatred with love and unity. Later, another person also hugged the man who pleaded guilty in one of the deadliest mass shootings in the U.S.

Crusius, a white community college dropout, had [posted online](https://substack.com/redirect/a7771a4e-d68e-47d2-b929-ddff6e088d70?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a screed about a Hispanic invasion of Texas before opening fire with an AK-style rifle at the store near the U.S.-Mexico border on Aug. 3, 2019. Crusius didn't address the families and survivors at his plea hearing Monday. He will serve multiple life sentences after pleading guilty to capital murder and 22 counts of aggravated assault with a deadly weapon.

"We would have opened our doors to you to share a meal, breakfast lunch or dinner, Mexican-style, so then your ugly thoughts of us that have been instilled in you would have turned around," Tinajero told him.

Tinajero said her brother, 60-year-old **Arturo Benavides** , was a "kind, sweet-hearted person," whose wife of over 30 years is broken hearted over her loss.

"Now she lives alone in their home full of memories that she can't forget," she said.

"I feel in my heart, to hug you very tight so you could feel my forgiveness, especially my loss, but I know it's not allowed," Tinajero said. "I want you to see and feel all of us who have been impacted by your actions."

Later, the judge asked her: "Ma'am, would it truly bring you peace and comfort if you could hug him?"

'Yes," she replied.

Her daughter, **Melissa Tinajero** , told reporters: "I don't know how she was able to do it. I could not do that. But she showed him something he could not show his victims."" [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/a2aa30f7-72e8-4698-95fa-7ad355fb64d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **#TXLEGE**

"**Texas hemp farmers fear full THC ban would nip industry in the bud,** " Texas Tribune's **Stephen Simpson** \-- "**Andrew Hill** , who studied and farmed hemp in California before it was legal in Texas, was a keynote speaker at the state's first-ever agricultural hemp expo in Dallas in 2019. Amid considerable hype and excitement that followed the recent federal legalization of hemp, Hill tried to warn farmers that the industry wasn't as profitable as state officials and seed vendors were pitching.

"There were guys running around telling farmers they could make $2,500 to $3,000 an acre on hemp. Being an actual farmer, not trying to sell seeds or clones, I couldn't help but burst out laughing," Hill said. "Everyone looked at me and asked what was so funny, and they gave me the mic and I said, 'Ladies and gentlemen, I'll tell you right now -- I haven't seen over $1,000 an acre since 2015.'"

Still, Texas lawmakers embraced the opportunity hemp presented in 2019, legalizing hemp products of the cannabis plant with less than 0.3% of THC, the psychoactive component in marijuana. Hill's Texas Star Hemp Farms was among those to make the investment necessary to profit from hemp, including owning almost all the means of production and sales and spending [millions](https://substack.com/redirect/02c7ac95-5874-4771-8063-4796c1467ab7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on seeds, licenses and facilities.

Six years after that initial rush, industrial hemp farming in Texas stands on the brink. [Senate Bill 3](https://substack.com/redirect/5a48bfb2-a94a-4e59-a70d-7f27ee653afc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which would ban any consumable hemp products that contain even trace amounts of THC, could destroy what farmers like Hill have built.

"Now, considering things like hemp hearts, hemp seed oils, salad dressings, and those health products that don't have any cannabinoids in them to get you high, [they] will still be illegal under this law," Hill said.

Hill is one of about 450 licensed hemp producers in the [Texas Industrial Hemp Program](https://substack.com/redirect/6d23f5f4-0137-4b78-8a8e-2504f91ae530?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at risk of losing a chunk of their livelihoods as Texas lawmakers have prioritized banning tetrahydrocannabinol, or THC products. Farmers say there is no way they can produce hemp without traces of THC, even for non-consumable products like clothing and paper, meaning SB 3 could deliver a death blow to the industry.

The GOP-controlled Legislature authorized the sale of consumable hemp a year after it was legalized nationwide to boost Texas agriculture by allowing the commercialization of hemp containing trace amounts of non-intoxicating delta-9 THC.

What ensued was a proliferation of hemp products, such as gummies, beverages, vapes and flower buds, sold at dispensaries and convenience stores across the state. Sen.**Charles Perry** , a Lubbock Republican who carried the 2019 hemp legalization bill, says such uses exploit a legal loophole and put children in danger.

His SB 3 attempts to correct this by penalizing violators who knowingly possess THC products with a misdemeanor that can carry up to a year in jail and 2 to 10 years in prison for manufacturing or selling them. The measure, which was approved by the Senate, also bars marketing and sales of consumable hemp to minors and requires all legal products to be sold in tamper-evident and child-resistant packaging.

Like SB 3, [House Bill 28](https://substack.com/redirect/98c5fda3-4a37-406b-8549-939227b53955?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) would ban synthetic THC and products like gummies and vapes. But the House's proposal focuses more on tightening regulatory loopholes, allowing hemp-infused beverages and assigning the alcohol industry to regulate products, as well as limiting the consumption of such products to those 21 years or older and implementing advertising regulations.

"This regulatory structure will also maintain the federal restriction on THC of no more than 0.3%, as well as limiting the amount a person can buy in a single day to 10 milligrams," said state Rep. **Ken King** , R-Canadian, sponsor of the bill.

If the House passes its proposal, the two chambers must reconcile their differences for the legislation to become law. The House hasn't taken up either of the hemp bills for a vote.

In public hearings, lawmakers heard from parents whose children were sickened by products containing dangerous unregulated forms of THC, and Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** said he will move to force an overtime session of the Legislature if lawmakers fail to pass the ban.

"Kids are getting poisoned today," Patrick told the Senate earlier this year." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/600e34d9-5bd7-4f92-978d-ad7184faa413?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

"**Does nuclear energy hold the key to Texas ' power needs? The state is exploring options,**" Dallas Morning News' **Philip Jankowski** \-- "A novel experiment underway in West Texas could help determine whether long-abandoned nuclear technology could be the future of Texas power.

At Abilene Christian University, a research lab is working to create the nation's first nuclear research reactor in more than 40 years. The intent is to prove small modular reactors are a clean, reliable energy source.

"Our goal is to take this technology and bless the world with it," said **Rusty Towell** , an engineering professor at ACU who leads the university's effort to build a small nuclear reactor.

Nuclear power -- once panned as overpriced, overregulated and dangerous -- is in the midst of a resurgence, overcoming stigmas associated with disasters at Chernobyl, Three Mile Island and Fukushima. Texas has noticed, and ACU's NEXT Lab isn't riding the nuclear wave alone.

Gov. **Greg Abbott** has signaled strong support for the technology after signing off on recommendations that included a $5 billion fund to develop nuclear power in Texas.

"Texas is the energy capital of the world, and we are ready to be No. 1 in advanced nuclear power," Abbott [said in November](https://substack.com/redirect/d5245b3c-3208-4d19-bae6-ff0d50a5d371?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

The CEO who oversees the Texas power grid, **Pablo Vegas** of ERCOT, sees vast potential for a fast-growing state hustling to meet future power demands.

"I'm very positive about nuclear," Vegas said in a recent interview. "I'm looking forward to seeing the momentum that has started this last year continue into 2025."

Nuclear reactors check nearly all the boxes for bipartisan support. Democrats are supportive of an industry that emits virtually no greenhouse gases. Republicans tend to favor nuclear's ability to produce power that is not affected by weather, unlike solar and wind.

In short, much like batteries, nuclear power can provide relief on two critical fronts. It is a way to slow climate change and its effects, including more frequent devastating weather events and the potential for severe drought. It also is a stable energy source needed to address population growth.

Advocates of nuclear power see Texas as a potential tipping point for a long-dormant energy sector.

Still, the power source does carry risk, including the release of radioactivity in a major emergency, such as the 1986 reactor explosion in Chernobyl or the 2011 tsunami that damaged containment in three reactors in Fukushima, Japan.

The promise of small modular reactors is clouded by financial uncertainty, doubts about their reliability, slow deployment timelines and unresolved nuclear waste issues.

"This technology is unproven ... and it may not deliver results to Texas for six to 10 years," **John Umphress** , a representative of the consumer advocacy group Public Citizen, said at a recent legislative hearing.

In Texas, nuclear reactors generate power at two sites -- Comanche Peak, about 45 miles southwest of Fort Worth, and the South Texas Project, about 100 miles northeast of Corpus Christi. Together, the sites' four nuclear reactors can power about 1.3 million homes.

One other reactor is in the works as Dow Inc. moves forward with plans to build a nuclear power plant at [its Seadrift site](https://substack.com/redirect/b587a50f-22d0-4de0-941a-867cb89e5b62?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) along the Texas coast.

ACU hit a significant milestone in September when [federal regulators approved](https://substack.com/redirect/4cc6a1f6-cc5a-4d4d-95ee-fd0be7ad704b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) its proposal to test a novel small modular reactor design on campus. Instead of connecting to the power grid, its reactor will serve as a research vehicle to prove whether its concept -- a molten salt reactor -- is viable.

The small reactor, only the second of its kind to receive a permit from federal nuclear regulators, uses a liquid salt mix to cool nuclear fuel instead of the more conventional water mixtures seen in large reactors.

The project is being developed by the university and Natura Resources, a company led by longtime oilman Douglas Robison, who gave more than $30 million to the university to create its advanced nuclear lab.

The Texas A&M University System also is creating [a nuclear power proving ground](https://substack.com/redirect/8bc4609c-69fd-4dd9-9911-83f9c4c10d82?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on university land in Bryan to test tech similar to what the NEXT Lab is developing in Abilene.

Texas A&M Chancellor**John Sharp** said massive projected increases in electricity demand drove him to pursue making the system's 2,300-acre RELLIS Campus a federally approved site for nuclear power.

"What is your answer to the AI power demand, you know, doubling over the next few decades or less than that?" Sharp said. "What's your answer to, you know, 1,000 people moving into Texas every day? What's your answer to all this? I mean, there is no answer except for nuclear power."

The university offers an enticing location because it is tackling the cumbersome and lengthy process of acquiring a site permit from the Nuclear Regulatory Commission. Projects selected for the site would still need federal approval of their reactor design, but having a preapproved site clears a major regulatory hurdle.

A&M announced in February it had picked four companies for the site, including Natura Resources. Sharp said a reactor could be switched on as early as 2030. ...

Federal regulators have approved only three reactors for power production in the past 28 years. With so few projects, domestic manufacturing and mining industries supporting a nuclear power supply chain have decayed.

The only large nuclear plant developed in the U.S. in the past decade faced [billions in cost overruns](https://substack.com/redirect/b9281e1f-e612-40fd-8553-07ab11fb42e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and major delays. By the time the Georgia nuclear facility Plant Vogtle switched on its first reactor, the project was seven years overdue and $17 billion over budget.

"The U.S. nuclear energy, essentially, has atrophied," said **Jay Yu** , president of uranium enrichment company LIS Technologies. "We've just been sitting around, not really building up our capabilities."

Most of the uranium used in domestic power production is imported from Canada and Kazakhstan. Russia had been a leading source, but the U.S. banned Russian uranium after the country invaded Ukraine in 2022.

In South Texas, two companies have leased 10 sites for uranium mining, [Inside Climate News](https://substack.com/redirect/7eb3d312-bc2c-4639-81f1-0bc7d9e80ef7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reported in December. However, those have largely remained dormant over the past 10 years, despite regulatory approval to begin mining from the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality.

That could change if lofty goals for nuclear power are realized.

In addition to Abbott's support for the industry, former President **Joe Biden** set a goal for nuclear power production to triple by 2050, and Trump has said he wants to limit regulations that slow the building process.

Industry experts said the most important signals for a nuclear jump start will come from the government. In Texas, that could take the form of a $2 billion fund of taxpayer cash to bring new nuclear generation to Texas' power grid by 2035.

House Bill 14 proposes creating a series of grant programs to encourage research and construction of small modular nuclear reactors. The bill is a priority of House Speaker **Dustin Burrows,** R-Lubbock, and is headed toward a vote in the House in coming weeks.

The proposal from Rep. **Cody Harris** , R-Palestine, has bipartisan support, but has a funding structure that would require an amendment to the Texas Constitution, leaving the ultimate decision up to voters.

Dallas Democratic Rep. **Rafael Anch ia** said that while enthusiasm is high, public support for nuclear remains fragile.

"You're one bad headline away from a collapse of public support," Anchia said." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/7b10ef9b-041e-4507-b526-568847e03823?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **LOCAL GOVERNMENT**

**" Dallas' $1.65 billion plan to rebuild Interstate 345 is still unfunde**d," Dallas Morning News' **Amber Gaudet** -- "A [proposed $1.65 billion rebuild](https://substack.com/redirect/c62b334a-34b7-4d43-ad81-02680e645fb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Interstate 345 in Dallas remains unfunded more than a decade after the Texas Department of Transportation first took a look at the project.

The agency plans to rebuild the highway that runs between downtown and Deep Ellum in a below-grade trench with new street overpasses above. TxDOT officials have said the project will accommodate future growth in the region and help mitigate maintenance costs for the aging stretch of roadway, which carries more than 180,000 vehicles daily.

Despite enthusiasm for the rebuild of the 51-year-old roadway, however, no funds have yet been committed to the project, TxDOT officials told the Dallas Transportation and Infrastructure Committee Tuesday.

"We do not have any funding on the project to date so that will take us time to find that funding," TxDOT representative **Ceason Clemens** said. "I'd expect it to take at least 3 to 5 years just to find that magnitude of funding."

A 2012 feasibility study first examined the 2.8-mile stretch of elevated highway from Interstate 30 to Woodall Rodgers Freeway. A series of follow-up studies and meetings with the City of Dallas, including 2023 Dallas City Council approval of the project, followed.

The project would restitch Deep Ellum and southern Dallas with the city center. Locals have pointed to the highway as an example of government-sanctioned segregation, where freeways bisected or walled off historically Black and Hispanic neighborhoods like Deep Ellum.

Committee members on Tuesday commended the agency for extensive community outreach for the project, including two public hearings this week.

TxDOT staff made several changes to plans based on public feedback. Those include removing a planned highway connection onto Allen Street after the nearby neighborhood raised concerns about traffic impacts, and reconfiguring Cesar Chavez Boulevard.

"You've not only gone out to the community and listened to them, you actually then made changes based on what you heard...so thank you for that part," councilmember **Carla Mendelsohn** said Tuesday.

"I don't know where you're going to find the $1.6 billion dollars, but I do think that when we get to construction, it's going to be difficult, having lived through a lot of construction in my district over the past couple years. When it's concentrated like this in one area, it's really hard."

Environmental assessment is expected to be completed by the end of summer 2025, according to TxDOT." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/a64a7e8c-039f-4714-ac27-a05fd549f1e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
